A real timeline of how BugRecon turns a cert log entry into a paid critical.

00:00 a TLS cert is issued.
01:30 the bounty is yours.

90 seconds, end to end. 🧵👇

00:00.
Target program adds preprod-api.example.com.
The team requests a TLS cert from Let's Encrypt.
The cert lands in the public Certificate Transparency log.

You did not see it. CertStream did.

00:08.
BugRecon sees the new cert hits a wildcard you monitor.
A new subdomain entry is created in your scope.

Your phone gets a Discord ping. You ignore it.

00:14.
Your automation rule kicks in:
new sub → port scan → HTTP probe → Nuclei.

All async. All queued. Zero clicks from you.

00:47.
Nuclei catches a known unauth RCE template hit on /api/exec.
Output captured. Curl PoC ready.

01:12.
Discord alert: 💥 Critical · RCE · preprod-api.example.com.
You open the report template, paste the PoC, attach the BugRecon evidence link, submit.

01:30. Triaged. Critical. Paid.

🚀 Stop checking certs by hand. Start cascading them into bounties.

#bugbounty #AI #recon

Most hunters do recon in steps:

1. Run subdomain scan
2. Wait
3. Run HTTP probe
4. Wait
5. Run nuclei
6. Wait
7. Forget about it

BugRecon does it as a chain reaction. One trigger, full pipeline. 🧵👇

The trigger can be anything:
• A new cert via CertStream
• A scope update from H1 / YWH / BC / Intigriti
• A recursive subdomain discovery
• A manual click

What happens next is where the magic lives.

The cascade you configure once:

port scan → HTTP probe → vuln scan → leaks scan → URL scan → basic auth → screenshot → takeover check → JS secret scan

All async. All queued. All fed by the previous step.

Every newly discovered wildcard gets auto-added to CertStream monitoring.

Recursive enum kicks off again on fresh subs.

Your attack surface map literally grows itself while you sleep.

And the punchline: every interesting finding lands in Discord with the context you need to triage.

No tabs. No babysitting. No missed crit.

🚀 Stop doing recon in steps. Start doing it in cascade.

Free tier → https://app.bugrecon.me
Discord → https://discord.gg/fQfjWMrxU5

#bugbounty

If you defend a perimeter, you have 3 problems:

1. You do not know every asset you expose
2. You do not know when new ones appear
3. You learn about exposures from a hunter, not your own tools

EASM fixes this. Here is how. 🧵👇

External Attack Surface Management means: continuously map what the internet sees of you, and flag every change.

Most tools do step 1. Few do step 2 in real time. Almost none give you evidence you can hand to engineering.

BugRecon doubles as an EASM platform:

• CertStream catches new subs the moment a cert is issued
• Scan diffs prove what changed between runs
• Centralized logs with per-scan drilldown
• Keycloak SSO ready, RBAC, multi-user

Your engineering team gets:
• A clean diff: "yesterday vs today"
• Real evidence (screenshots, headers, vuln output)
• Exportable scan history for audit

No more "trust me bro, you have shadow IT".

Same engine, two audiences:
• Hunters move fast and ship reports
• Security teams hold the line and prove it

One platform. Two outcomes. Same low infra cost.

🚀 Stop discovering your assets through bug bounty reports. Start mapping them yourself.

Talk to us → https://bugrecon.me/contact
App → https://app.bugrecon.me

Built by hunters, for hunters.

5 features your hand-rolled recon stack does not have.

If you have built one, you already know which ones hurt the most. 🧵👇

1. Real-time scope sync.

When a program adds a wildcard at noon, you find out at midnight (if at all).
BugRecon syncs H1, YWH, Bugcrowd, Intigriti and queues scans on the diff.

2. CertStream wired to a cascade.

Watching CT logs is easy. Catching a new sub, scanning it, and pinging you with a finding in under 2 minutes is not.
BugRecon does the whole chain.

3. Scan diffs you can read in 5 seconds.

Most stacks dump JSON. BugRecon shows you "yesterday vs today" with delta highlights. Engineering teams love this.

4. Nuclei spraying across multi-program scope.

A single template, fired across every live host in every program at once. Nobody is doing this from a Makefile.

5. Async Discord alerts that include enough context to triage from your phone.

Most homemade bots ping with "scan done". BugRecon pings with the finding.

🚀 Stop maintaining infra. Start hunting.

Free tier → https://app.bugrecon.me
Discord → https://discord.gg/fQfjWMrxU5

The most underrated bounty source in 2026 is still the same: hardcoded secrets in JavaScript bundles.

Devs ship them. Scanners miss them. Hunters who automate it eat well. 🧵👇

Why is it underrated?
• Bundles are minified and look intimidating
• grep'ing a 4MB main.chunk.js by hand is painful
• Most scanners flag 200 false positives per file

So most hunters skip it. Bad call.

BugRecon JS Analysis crawls every JS bundle on every live host in your scope.

Hidden endpoints. API keys. AWS / GCP creds. JWT secrets.

AI filters the noise so you only see what is actually exploitable.

Real flow:
new sub via CertStream → HTTP probe → JS scan → AWS key found in main.chunk.js → high severity Discord alert.

You go from cert log entry to leaked AWS access key in one cascade.

🚀 Stop skipping JS recon. Start auto-mining it.

Free tier → https://app.bugrecon.me
Discord → https://discord.gg/fQfjWMrxU5

Built by hunters, for hunters.

#bugbounty #jsrecon #secrets

A day in the life of a bug bounty hunter who automated their recon.

Spoiler: there is a lot less terminal and a lot more coffee. 🧵👇

07:00.
Phone buzz: Discord alert.
Overnight CertStream caught 4 new subs across 2 programs.
Cascade ran. 1 panel exposed, 1 takeover candidate.

Coffee while reading the evidence.

08:30.
Open the BugRecon dashboard.
Search: "apache 2.4.49"
3 hosts across 2 programs.
Click. Spray Nuclei. 2 minutes.

10:00.
Bugcrowd program updated scope: 3 new wildcards.
Already synced. Already queued. Already enumerating.

You did nothing. The pipeline did everything.

13:00.
Lunch.
Discord ping: leaked AWS key found in JS bundle on a synced YWH program.
You write the report on your phone.

16:00.
Open scan diffs from this morning.
Yesterday: 412 live hosts.
Today: 419.
7 new attack surface entries to triage. None of them required a manual scan.

22:00.
You go to bed.
CertStream is awake. Cascade is armed. Discord is loud.

Tomorrow, repeat.

🚀 Stop doing recon. Start receiving it.

Free tier → https://app.bugrecon.me
Discord → https://discord.gg/fQfjWMrxU5

11 scanners.
1 dashboard.
0 CLI to install.

This is what BugRecon ships out of the box for bug bounty recon.

Thread 🧵👇

🔍 Subdomain enumeration
Passive + active engines, DNS validation, wildcard filtering. No more babysitting 6 different tools.

🌐 HTTP probe + 🔌 Port scan
Live hosts on any port range. Custom headers, User-Agent, redirects. Real-time results piped straight to your scope view.

🕸 URL discovery + 🟨 JS analysis
Wayback, Common Crawl, active crawl. Then we crack JS bundles for hidden endpoints, API keys, hardcoded secrets. AI filters the noise.

⚡ Nuclei vuln scan
Built-in, community, custom templates. Spray a single CVE template across your entire scope in one click.

🕵️ Leak detection + 🏴 Subdomain takeover + 🔑 Basic auth spray
Dangling DNS, leaked credentials, default logins. The boring wins that actually pay rent.

📷 Screenshots + 🤖 AI analysis
Visual recon of every live host. AI ranks targets by exploitability so you know where to strike first.

🚀 Stop juggling 12 CLIs. Start hunting.

Free tier → https://app.bugrecon.me
Discord → https://discord.gg/fQfjWMrxU5

We built BugRecon because we got tired of duct-taping 12 CLI tools every time we joined a new program.

Now we ship it in public. Here is what that looks like. 🧵👇

Every new scanner ships with:
• A docs page (docs.bugrecon.me)
• A changelog tweet
• A demo in our Discord

If it breaks, we hear about it within the hour. If it slaps, we double down.

We run every sync against our own bug bounty programs first.

H1, YWH, Bugcrowd, Intigriti. All live. If the diff misses a domain, we feel it before you do.
Roadmap is ready. Lots of new scanners and features are in the starting blocks.

Come hang out:
• Break the product
• Ask for a scanner that does not exist yet
• Share a workflow we should automate
• Roast the dashboard if it deserves it

🚀 Stop watching from the sidelines. Start shaping it.
